original: You decide to ask Tanis about his job. Was there a special reason why he became a scout? The leithan shrugs — a motion that is mirrored by his reptilian back. “I’m afraid there isn’t much of a story behind it. I was a hunter back home, and I love the feel of braving the unknown, so this job was a perfect fit for me.” Your eyes are drawn to the trophies hanging from the wall behind him. You can’t even identify most of the beasts, but each and every one looks vicious: full of sharp teeth, claws, or both. Not a speck of dust can be found on them; it’s clear that Tanis takes pride in his hunting skills. Still, you give him a puzzled look, not satisfied with such a short reply. As far as you know, most leithans who leave their homeworld are looking for peaceful environs, not a Rush planet filled with potential dangers. The scout muses for a moment, raising a hand to his chin and looking up in an almost clichéd manner. “You’re not wrong. My home, Leitha, is a very hostile place, even after all the technology we’ve received from the U.G.C. To most leithans, the supposed ‘peace and quiet’ of a core world sounds like paradise.” And yet Tanis came to a Rush planet, of all places. Is he addicted to adrenaline or something? The scout chuckles. “I don’t know, maybe a little. Have you watched kaiju movies? That’s not too far from our reality back home. I’ve had to run and hide from beasts larger than any building you’ll find in this settlement. Nothing I’ve seen here in Mhen’ga can compare, so this place is pretty peaceful by my reckoning!” You give him a slow nod, now that you have a better grasp of his perspective. “What about you, [pc.mfn |Mr |Ms |Ser ]Steele? If you don’t mind me asking. It’s not every day you see the heir of a megacorp come to a frontier planet[pc.mfn | himself | herself | themself ], least of all as a rusher!” he says in an appreciative tone, his eyes taking stock of your gear. You shrug and proceed to tell him about your circumstances, as well as your quest. Tanis listens attentively, and by the end of it, he’s practically glowing with admiration. It’s pretty obvious that he has an even higher opinion of you now. The fact that he took everything you said at face value... It actually makes you a little worried. Does he have a good eye for people, or is he just a himbo? “I’ll be rooting for you, [pc.mfn |Mr |Ms |Ser ]Steele! Please let me know if there’s anything this scout can help you with,” the tauric man offers, with a heartfelt smile. You pause for a moment, trying to discern if there is a deeper meaning behind his words. The himbo’s smile is very warm, but you still can’t tell if he meant it just professionally, or if his interest in you goes beyond it... Either way, it’s time to change the topic.

original: You tell Tanis you’d like to know more about him. “I’m honored by your interest, [pc.mfn |Mr |Ms |Ser ]Steele, but I’m a simple scout. There isn’t much to tell.” Wait, is that a bit of a blush on his cheeks? This guy is really transparent... You smirk at him — come on, there’s gotta be more than that. What was his life like growing up? Does he have any siblings? “I do! Five clutch-sisters.” You blink. So, he was the only male in the clutch? “Yeah, and I’m the youngest,” the leithan starts, sheepishly. “Mom says everyone was worried because my egg was taking too long to hatch; my newborn sisters didn’t know left from right but they instinctively huddled up around to keep me warm until the shell finally cracked.” Wow. What was it like to grow up as the only boy in the club? The scout chuckles. “Well, I’d be lying if I said I wasn’t at least a bit pampered. They took me everywhere I wanted to go, got me delicious food, played all the games I liked...” Your mouth turns into a thin line as you can’t help but contrast this with the relative solitude of your childhood: Dad getting stuck at work, distant relatives, no siblings to play with... Of course, you don’t outright say any of this, but something in your expression must have tipped Tanis off, as his mouth shifts into an awkward smile. “It wasn’t all good though. Don’t get me wrong, I love my sisters more than anything, but they could be really overprotective... Took me ages to get my first girlfriend because no one was good enough for them. Not that it stopped me from fooling around. Came a point they wouldn’t let me leave the house without at least two of them to keep an eye on me.” You chuckle. That must have been rough. “Can’t blame them, though — I was a handful; always playing with dangerous things.” He turns slightly to show you a large, faded scar at the base of his tail, in a small area not protected by chitinous plates. “This was from trying to feed a wild rockbird when I was twelve; it started chasing me after the food ran out,” he chuckles as if that was nothing. It’s funny how people can sometimes look at the past and laugh at painful memories. “When I said I wanted to join the hunters’ guild, they almost had a heart attack. If Mom hadn’t laid down the word, I might have been forced to pick a different occupation...” ...and then you wouldn’t have been able to meet Tanis. That’s a sad thought. But say, doesn’t Tanis miss his family? He speaks of them fondly, despite everything. A complex mix of emotions crosses the leithan’s face. “I’d be lying if I said I didn’t, but at the same time, they’re the reason I decided to leave home in the first place. I felt like I would be their ‘precious baby brother’ forever if I stayed.” You nod sympathetically, having gone through a similar separation ritual when you left the nest to work as a [pc.class]. That was the last time you saw him in person... Trying to shift things in a lighter direction, you point out that Tanis seems to be doing well for himself on Mhen’ga. The scout smiles warmly. “I dare say I am! The pay is decent, and the locals are super friendly. If you get what I mean,” the blond himbo winks. You chuckle at that. Maybe you should ask him to show you around one of these days. “I would love to!”
